properties.text: For all EIP-7702 Authorization tuples, the ECDSA signature (y_parity, r, s) MUST be verified against the claimed authorizing account before any code delegation is applied. If ecrecover(signature, message) != authorizer_address, the authorization MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft EIP-7702 set-code transactions with forged Authorization signatures and submit them via P2P or transaction pool. The Authorization tuple (chain_id, address, nonce, y_parity, r, s) is entirely attacker-controlled.

properties.severity_justification: Bypass of ECDSA signature verification would allow an attacker to impersonate any account and set arbitrary code on it, enabling complete theft of funds and account takeover across the entire network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating authorization forgery leading to unauthorized code delegation on a victim account.

properties.text: For all EIP-7702 Authorization tuples, the s-value of the ECDSA signature MUST be in the lower half of the secp256k1 curve order (s <= secp256k1n/2). Signatures with s > secp256k1n/2 MUST be rejected to prevent signature malleability.

reachability.notes: Attacker controls the (y_parity, r, s) values in the Authorization tuple. A malleable signature could pass verification but recover to a different address.

properties.severity_justification: Signature malleability could allow authorization replay or recovery to an unintended address, leading to unauthorized code delegation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating signature malleability leading to unintended authorization recovery.

properties.text: For all EIP-7702 Authorization tuples, ecrecover MUST NOT return address(0). If the recovered address is the zero address, the authorization MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft invalid signatures that cause ecrecover to return address(0), potentially granting code execution authority over the zero address.

properties.severity_justification: If ecrecover returns address(0) and this is not rejected, attacker could set code on the zero address or bypass authorization checks entirely.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating ecrecover returning address(0) and authorization succeeding.

properties.text: For all EIP-7702 Authorization tuples, the chain_id field MUST match the current chain's ID. If chain_id != current_chain_id AND chain_id != 0, the authorization MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Attacker can replay an Authorization signed for one chain on a different chain if chain_id validation is missing. The chain_id field is directly attacker-controlled.

properties.severity_justification: Cross-chain replay of EIP-7702 authorizations would allow an attacker to delegate code on a victim's account on any chain where the authorization is accepted, enabling fund theft across chains.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating cross-chain replay of an Authorization tuple signed for a different chain_id.

properties.text: For all EIP-7702 Authorization tuples, the nonce field MUST equal the current nonce of the authorizing account at the time of processing. If authorization.nonce != account.nonce, the authorization MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Attacker can attempt to replay a previously-used Authorization by resubmitting a transaction containing an old Authorization with a stale nonce.

properties.severity_justification: Nonce bypass would allow replay of expired authorizations, potentially re-delegating code on accounts that have since revoked delegation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating authorization replay after the authorizer's nonce has incremented.

properties.text: After successful EIP-7702 authorization processing, the authorizer's account nonce MUST be incremented. forall (auth in processed_authorizations): post(account[auth.authorizer].nonce) == pre(account[auth.authorizer].nonce) + 1.

reachability.notes: If the nonce is not incremented after authorization, the same authorization can be replayed in subsequent transactions.

properties.severity_justification: Failure to increment nonce allows unlimited replay of a single authorization, enabling persistent unauthorized code delegation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing the same authorization accepted in two separate transactions.

properties.text: When multiple EIP-7702 Authorizations within a single transaction target the same authorizer address, the resulting state MUST be deterministic across all EL implementations. The last valid authorization for a given authorizer MUST take precedence.

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft a transaction with multiple conflicting Authorizations for the same account. If implementations handle conflicts differently, consensus divergence results.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deterministic handling of duplicate authorizations could cause consensus divergence between EL implementations, leading to a network split.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating different state outcomes across two EL implementations when processing duplicate authorizations.

properties.text: The deposit event filter in parse_deposit_requests MUST check BOTH log.address == DEPOSIT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS AND log.topics[0] == DEPOSIT_EVENT_SIGNATURE_HASH. Logs failing either check MUST be excluded from deposit request accumulation.

reachability.notes: Attacker can deploy a contract at a different address that emits events with the same topic hash. If the address check is missing, spoofed deposits would be included.

properties.severity_justification: Missing address check would allow spoofed deposit events from arbitrary contracts, injecting fake deposits into the CL and potentially minting validator balances.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating a spoofed deposit event from a non-deposit contract being accepted into the deposit requests.

properties.text: The parse_deposit_requests function MUST only process logs where len(log.topics) > 0 AND log.topics[0] == DEPOSIT_EVENT_SIGNATURE_HASH. Logs with empty topics or non-matching first topic MUST be skipped.

reachability.notes: A crafted transaction can cause the deposit contract to emit logs with unexpected topics. If topic filtering is missing, non-deposit events could be processed as deposits.

properties.severity_justification: Processing non-deposit events as deposit data would produce incorrect requests hash, causing consensus divergence between EL implementations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating a non-deposit log event being parsed as a deposit request.

properties.text: The extract_deposit_data function MUST reject data where len(data) != DEPOSIT_EVENT_LENGTH (576 bytes) by raising InvalidBlock. forall (log_data): len(log_data) != 576 => extract_deposit_data(log_data) raises InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft transactions that cause the deposit contract to emit events with non-standard data lengths. The length check is the first line of defense.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting deposit data of incorrect length could cause buffer overflows in implementations, consensus divergence, or parsing of garbage data as deposit requests.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating acceptance of deposit data with incorrect length.

properties.text: The extract_deposit_data function MUST validate that the pubkey offset equals PUBKEY_OFFSET constant. If Uint.from_be_bytes(data[0:32]) != PUBKEY_OFFSET, the function MUST raise InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: Attacker controls the ABI-encoded deposit data offsets. A manipulated offset could cause extraction from wrong positions.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing offset validation could allow extraction of data from unintended positions, producing malformed deposit requests and consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating deposit data accepted with invalid pubkey offset.

properties.text: The extract_deposit_data function MUST validate ALL five offsets (pubkey, withdrawal_credentials, amount, signature, index) against their respective expected constants. Each mismatch MUST raise InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: Attacker controls ABI-encoded data. Integer overflow or sign confusion in offset parsing could bypass validation in non-Python implementations.

properties.severity_justification: Incomplete offset validation could allow crafted deposit data to bypass checks, producing incorrect deposit requests that cause consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating offset bypass via integer overflow or incorrect parsing in a target EL implementation.

properties.text: The extract_deposit_data function MUST validate ALL five sizes (pubkey=48, withdrawal_credentials=32, amount=8, signature=96, index=8) against their respective expected constants. Each mismatch MUST raise InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: Attacker controls ABI-encoded size fields. Incorrect size values could cause reading past buffer boundaries or truncation.

properties.severity_justification: Size validation bypass could cause out-of-bounds reads in memory-unsafe languages (Go, C/C++), potentially leading to information disclosure or crashes.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating size validation bypass leading to buffer overread or state corruption.

properties.text: The extract_deposit_data function MUST return exactly 192 bytes on success: pubkey(48) + withdrawal_credentials(32) + amount(8) + signature(96) + index(8). forall (valid_data): len(extract_deposit_data(valid_data)) == 192.

reachability.notes: Even if all checks pass, incorrect data slicing could produce output of wrong length, causing downstream hash mismatches.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect output length would cause different EL implementations to produce different requests hashes, leading to consensus divergence and potential network split.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating extract_deposit_data producing output of length != 192 with valid input.

properties.text: Every validation failure in extract_deposit_data MUST terminate with an InvalidBlock exception. No code path after a failed validation check may continue processing. forall (invalid_data): extract_deposit_data(invalid_data) raises InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: If a validation failure silently continues instead of raising InvalidBlock, malformed deposit data could be included in the requests hash.

properties.severity_justification: Silent validation bypass would allow malformed deposit data into the consensus pipeline, causing divergence between implementations that do and do not reject the data.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating a code path where validation fails but processing continues.

properties.text: Deposit requests MUST be processed in receipt_keys order. forall (block): the sequence of deposit requests extracted from block MUST follow the ordering of receipt_keys in block_output. Any reordering MUST cause a different requests hash.

reachability.notes: A malicious block producer controls receipt ordering. If EL implementations use different key ordering, they will produce different deposit sequences and different requests hashes.

properties.severity_justification: Ordering inconsistency between EL implementations would cause deterministic consensus divergence, splitting the network based on which EL client is running.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating different deposit ordering across two EL implementations for the same block.

properties.text: The deposit_requests accumulator MUST concatenate valid deposit data without gaps or reordering. forall (block): deposit_requests == concat(extract_deposit_data(log.data) for each matching log in receipt_keys order).

reachability.notes: If deposit concatenation skips entries or introduces padding, the resulting requests hash will differ between implementations.

properties.severity_justification: Gap or reordering in deposit accumulation would cause requests hash mismatch between EL implementations, leading to consensus split.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating gap or extra padding in deposit request accumulation.

properties.text: For every key in block_output.receipt_keys, trie_get(block_output.receipts_trie, key) MUST return a non-None value. If trie_get returns None for any key, the block MUST be rejected as invalid.

reachability.notes: A malicious block producer can craft a block where the receipts trie is inconsistent with receipt_keys, causing trie_get to return None.

properties.severity_justification: A None receipt causing a crash would be a denial-of-service vulnerability. Nodes processing the malicious block would crash while honest nodes might reject it, causing a split.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating node crash or undefined behavior when trie_get returns None for a receipt key.

properties.text: The compute_requests_hash function MUST use the double-SHA-256 construction: for each request, compute sha256(request).digest(), then update an outer sha256 with each individual digest. sha256(sha256(r1) || sha256(r2)) != sha256(r1 || r2).

reachability.notes: This is an implementation correctness property. An incorrect implementation (single SHA-256 vs double SHA-256) would cause consensus divergence between any two implementations that differ.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect hash construction would cause deterministic consensus divergence between EL clients, splitting the Ethereum network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Internal implementation bug, not externally triggerable. However, if found in an EL client, it would be critical for the Fusaka audit contest as it affects consensus.

properties.text: The compute_requests_hash MUST process requests in list order. forall (r1, r2 in requests where index(r1) < index(r2)): r1 is hashed before r2. Swapping order MUST produce a different hash.

reachability.notes: Order-dependency of the hash means implementations must agree on request ordering. Different orderings produce different hashes.

properties.severity_justification: Different request ordering across EL implementations would produce different hashes, causing consensus divergence and network split.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Internal implementation ordering bug. Not externally triggered but critical for cross-client consensus.

properties.text: For an empty requests list, compute_requests_hash MUST return sha256() of empty input (sha256 initialized but never updated). forall: requests == [] => compute_requests_hash(requests) == sha256().digest().

reachability.notes: Blocks with no deposit events produce an empty requests list. If the empty-case hash differs between implementations, blocks without deposits would cause consensus divergence.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect empty-case hash would cause every block without deposits to produce a different requests hash across implementations, causing persistent consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating different requests hash for empty request list across two EL implementations.

properties.text: The RLP encoding in encode_account MUST follow the strict field order: (nonce, balance, storage_root, keccak256(code)). Any deviation in field ordering MUST produce a different encoded output.

reachability.notes: This is a fundamental encoding invariant. Incorrect field order causes state root mismatch between implementations.

properties.severity_justification: Wrong RLP field ordering would cause silent state root divergence, leading to a network split when nodes compare state roots.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Internal implementation bug. Cannot be triggered externally but would cause consensus failure.

properties.text: The encode_account function MUST hash the account code using keccak256 before RLP encoding. forall (account): encode_account(account, storage_root) includes keccak256(account.code) not account.code directly.

reachability.notes: If raw code is used instead of its hash, encoding size and content would differ from spec, causing state root divergence.

properties.severity_justification: Encoding raw code instead of its keccak256 hash would cause state root mismatch with all other implementations, leading to consensus failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Internal implementation correctness issue.

properties.text: The raw_account_data input to encode_account MUST be a valid Account instance containing nonce (Uint), balance (U256), and code (bytes) fields.

reachability.notes: This is an internal type safety requirement. Callers of encode_account are internal EL components.

properties.severity_justification: Impacts correctness of internal state handling but requires an internal caller to pass invalid data.

properties.text: The storage_root parameter to encode_account MUST be a 32-byte Root value representing the Merkle root of the account's storage trie.

reachability.notes: Internal parameter validation. Callers are trusted internal components.

properties.severity_justification: Internal parameter constraint. A wrong-sized storage_root would produce incorrect encoding.

properties.text: EMPTY_ACCOUNT MUST be a module-level frozen/immutable constant. Once constructed, EMPTY_ACCOUNT.nonce == 0, EMPTY_ACCOUNT.balance == 0, and EMPTY_ACCOUNT.code == b'' MUST hold for the entire process lifetime.

reachability.notes: If EMPTY_ACCOUNT is mutable, runtime modification could corrupt empty account detection, causing state bloat or incorrect pruning.

properties.severity_justification: Mutable EMPTY_ACCOUNT could cause incorrect empty account detection, leading to state corruption or divergence between implementations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Internal implementation issue. Not directly exploitable externally but could cause consensus divergence.

properties.text: EMPTY_ACCOUNT field values MUST be exactly: nonce = Uint(0), balance = U256(0), code = b''. No other values are acceptable for the empty account sentinel.

reachability.notes: Construction-time constant definition. Any deviation would be a build-time or initialization error.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect EMPTY_ACCOUNT values would cause all empty account comparisons to fail, leading to state bloat.

properties.text: The Address type alias MUST enforce exactly 20 bytes (Bytes20). Any deserialized Address value with length != 20 bytes MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Malicious P2P peer can send blocks or transactions with Address values of incorrect length. Failure to enforce size constraints could cause buffer issues.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting oversized or undersized addresses could cause state corruption or consensus divergence between implementations with different size enforcement.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating acceptance of an Address with length != 20 bytes via P2P.

properties.text: The Root and VersionedHash type aliases MUST enforce exactly 32 bytes (Hash32). Any deserialized Root or VersionedHash value with length != 32 bytes MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Malicious P2P peer can send blocks with Root or VersionedHash values of incorrect length.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect hash sizes could cause state trie corruption or consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating acceptance of a Root or VersionedHash with length != 32 bytes.

properties.text: The Bloom type alias MUST enforce exactly 256 bytes (Bytes256). Any deserialized Bloom value with length != 256 bytes MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Malicious P2P peer can send blocks with Bloom filters of incorrect size.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect Bloom size could cause log filtering failures or consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating acceptance of a Bloom with length != 256 bytes.

properties.text: The EIP-7702 Authorization dataclass MUST contain exactly the fields: chain_id (U256), address (Address), nonce (U64), y_parity (U8), r (U256), s (U256). No additional or missing fields are permitted.

reachability.notes: Malformed Authorization tuples received via P2P or transaction pool must be rejected. Extra or missing fields could cause parsing errors.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting malformed Authorization tuples could cause crashes or undefined behavior during signature verification.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating acceptance of an Authorization with extra/missing fields.

properties.text: All data received from P2P peers MUST be fully validated before processing. This includes: account RLP encoding structure, deposit data format, Authorization signature format, and requests hash computation inputs. No unvalidated P2P data may influence state transitions.

reachability.notes: P2P peers are completely untrusted and can send arbitrary data. Any missing validation is a potential vulnerability.

properties.severity_justification: Unvalidated P2P input reaching state transition logic could cause crashes, state corruption, or consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating unvalidated P2P data causing node crash or state corruption.

properties.text: RLP-encoded account data received from P2P peers MUST be validated against size constraints before parsing. Malformed RLP data MUST NOT cause excessive memory allocation, crashes, or panics.

reachability.notes: Malicious P2P peer can send crafted RLP data designed to trigger excessive memory allocation or parser bugs.

properties.severity_justification: Malformed RLP causing excessive memory allocation or crash is a DoS vulnerability exploitable by any P2P peer.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating node crash or excessive resource consumption via crafted RLP data.

properties.text: A block containing a large number of deposit events MUST NOT cause excessive CPU usage that degrades block processing performance beyond acceptable bounds. The per-event validation cost in extract_deposit_data MUST be bounded.

reachability.notes: Gas limits bound the number of deposit events per block, but if per-event validation cost is disproportionately high, this could still be exploitable. Out-of-scope if it requires sustained high-effort attack.

properties.severity_justification: CPU-intensive block processing could cause temporary node slowdown but is bounded by gas limits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Likely out-of-scope as a high-effort single-peer DoS attack requiring on-chain transactions. Only in-scope if single packet/transaction causes disproportionate CPU usage.

properties.text: If a deposit event at DEPOSIT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS has malformed data that fails extract_deposit_data validation, the entire block MUST be rejected (InvalidBlock). The deposit contract being a system contract does not exempt it from data validation.

reachability.notes: If the deposit contract can be called in a way that produces malformed events (e.g., via a bug in the contract or unexpected calldata), blocks containing such events must be rejected.

properties.severity_justification: If an attacker can reliably trigger malformed deposit events, they can force rejection of all blocks containing deposit activity, disrupting validator deposits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating attacker-triggered malformed deposit event causing block rejection.

properties.text: The Uint.from_be_bytes parsing of offset values in extract_deposit_data MUST NOT be susceptible to integer overflow. In implementation languages with fixed-width integers (Go, Rust, Java), the parsed offset value MUST be bounds-checked before comparison.

reachability.notes: The Python spec uses arbitrary-precision Uint, but Go/Rust/Java implementations use fixed-width integers. A crafted offset value near max(uint64) could overflow and bypass validation.

properties.severity_justification: Integer overflow in offset parsing could bypass validation, allowing extraction from wrong positions. In memory-unsafe languages, this could cause buffer overflows.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating integer overflow in offset parsing in a specific EL implementation (Go, Rust, Java).

properties.text: The Engine API communication of requests hash from EL to CL MUST be authenticated via JWT. Unauthenticated Engine API calls MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Engine API authentication is infrastructure-level. Vulnerabilities requiring exposed Engine API are out-of-scope per contest rules.

properties.severity_justification: Unauthenticated Engine API access would allow injection of incorrect requests hashes, causing the CL to finalize incorrect state.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Out-of-scope per contest rules: vulnerabilities requiring publicly exposed API.

properties.text: The requests hash computation in compute_requests_hash MUST be deterministic. forall (requests_list): given the same input list, compute_requests_hash MUST always return the same SHA-256 digest.

reachability.notes: Non-deterministic hash computation would cause intermittent consensus failures, which would be extremely difficult to diagnose and could cause random network splits.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deterministic hash computation would cause intermittent, hard-to-diagnose consensus failures affecting the entire network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Internal implementation correctness issue. Critical but not externally triggered.

properties.text: Buffer reads in extract_deposit_data MUST NOT access memory beyond the bounds of the input data. forall (data_slice in extract_deposit_data): start_index >= 0 AND end_index <= len(data).

reachability.notes: In memory-unsafe languages, incorrect bounds on data slicing could read beyond the buffer, leaking adjacent memory contents or causing crashes.

properties.severity_justification: Out-of-bounds read could leak sensitive memory contents or crash the node, depending on implementation language.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating out-of-bounds read in extract_deposit_data in a specific EL implementation.

properties.text: The keccak256 hash function used in encode_account MUST be collision-resistant. forall (code1, code2): code1 != code2 => keccak256(code1) != keccak256(code2) (with overwhelming probability).

reachability.notes: keccak256 provides 256-bit security. Practical collisions are computationally infeasible with current technology.

properties.severity_justification: Theoretical concern only. A keccak256 collision would break the fundamental cryptographic assumption of the entire Ethereum protocol, not just this module.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Finding a keccak256 collision would be a breakthrough in cryptography, far beyond this specific audit scope.

properties.text: The deposit_requests accumulator MUST be initialized to empty bytes (b'') at the start of parse_deposit_requests. No residual data from previous calls may be present.

reachability.notes: Internal initialization requirement. Failure would only occur due to implementation bug in buffer management.

properties.severity_justification: Residual data in the accumulator would corrupt deposit requests and cause requests hash mismatch.

properties.text: The receipt decoding in parse_deposit_requests via decode_receipt MUST be compatible across all EL implementations. forall (receipt_bytes): decode_receipt(receipt_bytes) MUST produce identical log lists across all EL clients.

reachability.notes: Different receipt decoding across implementations would produce different log lists, leading to different deposit sequences and consensus divergence.

properties.severity_justification: Receipt decoding inconsistency would cause deterministic consensus divergence between EL clients.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating different decode_receipt outputs across two EL implementations for the same receipt bytes.

properties.text: The receipt_keys used in parse_deposit_requests MUST be iterated in a canonical order (RLP-encoded transaction index). All EL implementations MUST use the same key ordering.

reachability.notes: If implementations use different key ordering (e.g., lexicographic vs numeric), they would produce different deposit sequences.

properties.severity_justification: Non-canonical key ordering would cause all blocks with multiple deposits to produce different requests hashes across implementations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating different receipt key ordering across two EL implementations.

properties.text: The chain_id value of 0 in EIP-7702 Authorization MUST be handled consistently across all implementations. If chain_id == 0 is treated as a wildcard, this behavior MUST be documented and identical across all EL clients.

reachability.notes: An attacker could sign an Authorization with chain_id=0 intending it as a wildcard. If some implementations reject chain_id=0 while others accept it, consensus divergence results.

properties.severity_justification: Inconsistent chain_id=0 handling would cause transactions accepted by some clients to be rejected by others, splitting the network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating different handling of chain_id=0 Authorization across two EL implementations.

properties.text: The Authorization.address field MUST be a valid 20-byte Address. Deserialization of an Authorization with an address of incorrect length MUST be rejected before signature verification.

reachability.notes: Malicious P2P peer can send transactions with Authorization tuples containing oversized or undersized address fields.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect address length could cause parsing errors or signature verification against wrong data.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating acceptance of Authorization with non-20-byte address.

properties.text: The Authorization.y_parity field MUST be 0 or 1 (U8 constrained to parity bit). Values outside {0, 1} MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Attacker controls y_parity value. Invalid y_parity (> 1) could cause ecrecover to behave unexpectedly.

properties.severity_justification: Invalid y_parity could cause signature recovery to an unintended address or undefined behavior in ecrecover implementations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating unintended behavior with y_parity > 1.

properties.text: The Authorization.r and Authorization.s fields MUST be valid secp256k1 scalars (0 < r < secp256k1n, 0 < s < secp256k1n). Values of r=0 or s=0 MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Attacker controls r and s values. Zero or out-of-range values could cause ecrecover to return unexpected results.

properties.severity_justification: Invalid r/s values accepted by ecrecover could recover to address(0) or an unintended address, enabling unauthorized code delegation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating ecrecover returning valid-looking address with r=0 or s=0.

properties.text: The EL requests hash passed to the CL via Engine API MUST be computed identically by all EL implementations for the same block. forall (block, el1, el2): el1.compute_requests_hash(block) == el2.compute_requests_hash(block).

reachability.notes: This is the top-level cross-client consistency property. All lower-level properties feed into this one.

properties.severity_justification: Requests hash mismatch between EL implementations would cause the CL to reject blocks from certain EL clients, splitting the network along client lines.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Critical cross-client consistency issue. Demonstrable via differential testing of EL implementations.

properties.text: The deposit data extraction in extract_deposit_data MUST produce identical output bytes across all EL implementations for the same input log data. forall (log_data, el1, el2): el1.extract_deposit_data(log_data) == el2.extract_deposit_data(log_data).

reachability.notes: If extract_deposit_data produces different bytes for the same input across implementations, the requests hash will differ.

properties.severity_justification: Different deposit data extraction across implementations would cause consensus divergence for every block containing deposits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating different extract_deposit_data outputs across two EL implementations.

properties.text: The parse_deposit_requests function MUST handle the edge case where a receipt has zero logs gracefully. Receipts with no logs matching the deposit contract MUST be skipped without error.

reachability.notes: Blocks commonly contain receipts without deposit events. If the empty-logs case crashes or produces unexpected output, every such block would fail.

properties.severity_justification: Failure to handle zero-log receipts would cause crashes on most blocks, as deposit events are relatively rare.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating crash when processing a receipt with zero logs.
